work_unit: runner-exec/scripts/creds.js shell injection + empty catch blocks
situation: audit-2026-07-02-003 finding SK-RE-008 (high severity): getSessionToken uses execSync with interpolated dbPath in shell command string, enabling $(cmd) injection if HOME or path is adversarial. Co-located SK-RE-018: empty catch blocks at lines 14,22,30 swallow root cause making diagnosis impossible.
action: Derived fix from audit-2026-07-02-003. Replaced execSync(`sqlite3 "${dbPath}" ...`) with execFileSync('sqlite3', [dbPath, 'SELECT ...']) to bypass shell parsing entirely. Added console.warn with error code to all three empty catch blocks in getInternalSecret and getSessionToken. Added execFileSync to require destructuring.
outcome_evidence: commit 07c1327 on auto/opt-runner-exec-1782940040 pushed to origin; node --check syntax OK; git diff shows 11 insertions 5 deletions confined to creds.js
next_suggestion: Continue dispatching pending findings from audit-2026-07-02-003: SK-RE-006 (creds.js secret file permission check), SK-RE-007 (pgrep pattern too broad), SK-RE-010 (exec.js ws error handler missing clearTimeout). All are route-fix in same runner-exec area → can batch into same worktree.

audit_findings.remediation: Use execFileSync array form to bypass shell
